---
title: "Algorithms vs Moore's Law"
description: "Sherry and Thompson study 113 algorithm families since the 1940s — for medium and large problem sizes, 30%-43% improve faster than Moore's Law."
author: "synesis"
date: "2021-10-17"
categories: [computer science, AI, paper, links]
image: cover.jpg
---

![Silicon power vs brain power. Image from [IEEE Xplore](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/9540991).](cover.jpg){fig-align="center"}

Silicon power vs. brain power.

Yash Sherry and Neil Thompson from Massachusetts Institute of Technology studied 113 algorithm families since 1940s — that's 57 textbooks and 1,100 research papers — and see how fast they have improved compared to hardware/Moore's law: for medium to large problem sizes, 30% and 43% of them have improved faster than silicon.

Sherry, Yash, and Neil C. Thompson. 2021. "How Fast Do Algorithms Improve?" Proceedings of the IEEE, 1–10. <https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/9540991>

Algorithm Wiki: <http://algorithm-wiki.org>


*Originally posted on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/benjaminhan_algorithm-mooreslaw-activity-6855727494973259776--3ma).*
